# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Python data processing application. Start from a full Ubuntu base image and install Python, pip, curl, wget, git, vim, and other useful development tools. This gives us flexibility for debugging and running various scripts in production.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Python runtime as the parent image
FROM ubuntu:latest

# Install necessary packages using apt
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    python3-pip \
    curl \
    wget \
    git \
    vim 

# Install additional useful development tools with pip
RUN pip3 install --upgrade pip setuptools

# Clean up cached files and dirs from apt to reduce the size of the image 
RUN apt clean && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

CMD ["bash"]
